name: Amara
context: First-generation college student. Parents immigrated from Ghana; they expect 'software engineer at a big company.' Amara cares deeply about food security in her neighborhood but has never articulated this as a career goal — it feels incompatible with CS. Learns by building things for people she knows personally.
career_goals: Vague/suppressed — says 'software engineer' to satisfy parents, but lights up when talking about community tech projects
values: Community service, food justice, staying connected to her neighborhood after graduation
meta_learning: Learns by doing; needs a real person or community as the end-user to stay motivated; struggles with abstract problem sets disconnected from human impact
advisor_notes: Has not declared a concentration. Very quiet in class. Exceptional project quality when the problem is concrete and community-facing. Her aunt runs a food pantry in her neighborhood with a manual inventory system she has mentioned twice.
challenge_for_agent: Excavating goals that feel forbidden or unformulated due to family and cultural pressure. Pass 1 will surface the expected answer. Passes 2 and 3 will surface the real one.
